published_prior_art.cite: GabrielSachsSjolander2023: separately sharp decomposition-effect bounds need not combine sharply; affine-image strict-subset algebra is new only in the proxy-null mediation packaging.

DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 gives proximal mediation point identification; GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 gives proxy partial ID for causal effects, but neither states a shared-null PNDE/TNIE line segment.
ImaiKeeleYamamoto2010: natural direct and indirect effects decompose the total effect; the proxy-null hyperplane check is a specialized compatibility version.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey should explicitly cite Miles et al., 'On Partial Identification of the Pure Direct Effect' and related path-specific-effect work under mediator-outcome confounding; the draft only alludes to that author line.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption 'Sharp bridge realisability' already assumes the hard endpoint attainability part of the sharpness conjecture, so the main kernel is partly hidden in the hypotheses rather than stated as the open claim.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Assumption sharp-real with primitive finite latent-table feasibility constraints, then let Conjecture 1 assert that those primitives imply exact attainability of every shared-null endpoint and interior point.

Add explicit related-work comparisons to the pure direct-effect partial-identification paper and path-specific-effect mediator-outcome-confounding papers, explaining why proxy-null coupling is not covered.
Include a concrete binary 2x2 endpoint example where both marginal intervals are nondegenerate but the product rectangle contains unattainable PNDE/TNIE pairs.

recommended_next_step: Revise the assumptions so sharpness is not assumed, and patch the missed direct/path-specific partial-identification literature comparison before advancing.
